normalized_text:
Welcome to this lesson on loops. In this topic, sometimes the requirement will be, for example, if I use a for-loop with the numbers 10, 20, 30, and 40. When I print this list using a for-loop, it comes out as 10, 20, 30, and 40. We need to print it in a specific way where 40 should not be printed. The first requirement is that the code should only print certain elements and not the entire list. The second requirement is that even if there is no number in between, we need to handle these two options using if, else, elif, break, and continue.

For example, if I have a list of 10, 20, 30, 40, and 50, and I want to skip a particular value in the loop, what do I need? Let's check the break statement. At which point should we stop the code? We want to print 10, 20, and 30, but not 40. If 50 is printed, then I will use a break when 40 is encountered. In the list, only 40 should be skipped.

For loops, while loops, if, else, elif, break, continue, return, list, tuple, dict, set, function, variable, string, integer, boolean, loop, condition, index, append, range, class. If x is equal to 40, when the value of x reaches 40, I will write a break in my code. If this code block is true, then only 40 and 50 will not be printed. If the code is false, 10, 20, or 30 will get printed.

If else, elif, break, continue, return, list, tuple, dict, set, function, variable, string, index, append, range, class, import module, error, exception. Consider a list from 10 to 50. For loop, x is equal to 40. If true, it will break; otherwise, it will print. Now the code has been broken. There is no condition that I have only numbers; here, I can take a string. If L is equal to a certain value, then it is your condition. Set a, b, and c. This is multiple strings in the code. If else, elif, break, continue, return, list, tuple, dict, set, function, variable, string, index, append, range, class. If x is equal to mango, if I run, see orange, apple, and for mango, the code is breaking. This is how break works. Break means stopping the loop, but what do we know now? Let's see next, continue.

Next, use continue to skip a particular value in the code block. For example, skip 30 using continue. The rest works the same, so you can use continue in place of break, and the code will print 10, 20, and 40. Apply the same code continuously. I want apple to come in orange and mango. If else, elif, break, continue, return, list, tuple, dict, set, function, variable, string, integer, boolean, loop, condition. See guys, orange, mango, apple. Apple is skipped, and if I remove it from the list, fruits will be modified. Here, I will use a list, and when it is not in the list, I have to skip 40 or 30. So from this list, 10 and 20 have to be printed, then 40 and 50 have to be printed.

To do that, x is equal to 30. Continue. If I run the sequence 10, 20, 40, 50, then break. Otherwise, print the value and break. If continue, print the remaining value. In the video, I explained this concept. Please subscribe, share the video with your friends, and join our coding community. In the next tutorial, we will explore more concepts. Thank you, and take care.

[TOPIC_SHIFT]

In the next tutorial, we will delve into more advanced topics.
topic_shifts: Introduction to loop requirements and skipping elements
Explanation of break statement and its usage
Discussion on handling strings and conditions in loops
Introduction to continue statement and its application
Invitation to subscribe and join the community
